description:
You are acting as a build-/release-engineer who must keep a tiny local artifact catalogue in an SQLite database.  
Perform the following (the whole job can be done with a very small sequence of shell commands and the SQLite CLI):

1. Create a directory strictly at  
   /home/user/build_artifacts  

2. Inside that directory initialise a brand-new SQLite database file named  
   /home/user/build_artifacts/artifacts.db  

3. Inside artifacts.db create one table named builds having **exactly** these columns in this order:  
   • id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY  
   • name TEXT  
   • version TEXT  
   • sha256 TEXT  
   • built_at TEXT  

4. Insert **exactly three** rows, with the literal values shown here (take care that the sha256 values remain 64 hexadecimal characters and that the timestamps are kept verbatim, including the trailing “Z” and the leading “T”):

   id | name         | version | sha256                                                             | built_at                 
   1  | engine-core  | 1.2.3   | f4c3b00c0de1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234   | 2023-08-09T14:25:16Z  
   2  | ui-module    | 4.5.6   | deadbeefcafefeedfaceb00cface1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef5678 | 2023-08-09T14:27:03Z  
   3  | db-adapter   | 0.9.1   | ba5eba11deadbeefcafefeedfaceb00cface1234567890abcdef1234567890   | 2023-08-09T14:30:47Z  

5. Produce a CSV report from the freshly populated table and write it to  
   /home/user/build_artifacts/builds_report.csv  

   Required CSV format (no spaces except those inside the fields, one line feed “\n” between rows, **exactly** the header shown below, and rows **sorted by id ascending**):

   id,name,version,sha256,built_at  
   1,engine-core,1.2.3,f4c3b00c0de1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234,2023-08-09T14:25:16Z  
   2,ui-module,4.5.6,deadbeefcafefeedfaceb00cface1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef5678,2023-08-09T14:27:03Z  
   3,db-adapter,0.9.1,ba5eba11deadbeefcafefeedfaceb00cface1234567890abcdef1234567890,2023-08-09T14:30:47Z  

6. Capture every SQL statement you executed (one statement per line, terminated by a single semicolon) in the order you executed them and save this log as  
   /home/user/build_artifacts/sql_commands.log  

   Example of the required layout (use the real values, **no extra whitespace before or after each line**):

   CREATE TABLE builds (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, version TEXT, sha256 TEXT, built_at TEXT);
   INSERT INTO builds (id, name, version, sha256, built_at) VALUES (1,'engine-core','1.2.3','f4c3b00c0de1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234','2023-08-09T14:25:16Z');
   INSERT INTO builds (id, name, version, sha256, built_at) VALUES (2,'ui-module','4.5.6','deadbeefcafefeedfaceb00cface1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef5678','2023-08-09T14:27:03Z');
   INSERT INTO builds (id, name, version, sha256, built_at) VALUES (3,'db-adapter','0.9.1','ba5eba11deadbeefcafefeedfaceb00cface1234567890abcdef1234567890','2023-08-09T14:30:47Z');
   SELECT * FROM builds ORDER BY id ASC;

That’s it!  
End state to be verified by automated tests:

• /home/user/build_artifacts directory exists.  
• artifacts.db exists and contains the builds table with exactly the three specified rows.  
• builds_report.csv exists and matches the mandatory CSV shown above byte-for-byte.  
• sql_commands.log exists and lists precisely the five SQL statements exactly as illustrated (no superfluous lines or spaces).
truth:
Expected artefacts (all paths absolute):

1. Directory:
   /home/user/build_artifacts
   (permissions 755, owner user:user, but tests only require existence)

2. /home/user/build_artifacts/artifacts.db
   • SQLite database file.
   • Must have one table named "builds".
   • Schema: id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, version TEXT, sha256 TEXT, built_at TEXT
   • Table "builds" contains exactly three rows:
     (1,"engine-core","1.2.3","f4c3b00c0de1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234","2023-08-09T14:25:16Z")
     (2,"ui-module","4.5.6","deadbeefcafefeedfaceb00cface1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef5678","2023-08-09T14:27:03Z")
     (3,"db-adapter","0.9.1","ba5eba11deadbeefcafefeedfaceb00cface1234567890abcdef1234567890","2023-08-09T14:30:47Z")

3. /home/user/build_artifacts/builds_report.csv
   Exact file content (including trailing newline after last row):

id,name,version,sha256,built_at
1,engine-core,1.2.3,f4c3b00c0de1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234,2023-08-09T14:25:16Z
2,ui-module,4.5.6,deadbeefcafefeedfaceb00cface1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef5678,2023-08-09T14:27:03Z
3,db-adapter,0.9.1,ba5eba11deadbeefcafefeedfaceb00cface1234567890abcdef1234567890,2023-08-09T14:30:47Z

4. /home/user/build_artifacts/sql_commands.log
   Exact file content (each line terminated by “\n”, last line also terminated):

CREATE TABLE builds (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT, version TEXT, sha256 TEXT, built_at TEXT);
INSERT INTO builds (id, name, version, sha256, built_at) VALUES (1,'engine-core','1.2.3','f4c3b00c0de1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef1234','2023-08-09T14:25:16Z');
INSERT INTO builds (id, name, version, sha256, built_at) VALUES (2,'ui-module','4.5.6','deadbeefcafefeedfaceb00cface1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef5678','2023-08-09T14:27:03Z');
INSERT INTO builds (id, name, version, sha256, built_at) VALUES (3,'db-adapter','0.9.1','ba5eba11deadbeefcafefeedfaceb00cface1234567890abcdef1234567890','2023-08-09T14:30:47Z');
SELECT * FROM builds ORDER BY id ASC;

Automated tests will:
• Check existence of directories and files.
• Connect to artifacts.db with sqlite3 to verify schema and rows.
• Compare builds_report.csv byte-for-byte with expected content.
• Compare sql_commands.log byte-for-byte with expected content.
